files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 9587fa1..d768e28 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,78 +1,66 @@
 # NovaMind AI - Strategy Memory
 
-## Current State (Week 18, Day 126)
-- Cash: $98,935
-- Subscribers: 560 (S1=550, S2=8, S3=2)
-- Plan distribution: A=166, B=394, C=0
-- Revenue: $5,515/month ($183.83/day avg from billing spread)
-- Weekly costs: $9,884/week ($1,412/day avg)
-- Net burn: ~$1,227/day
-- Cash runway: ~71 days
+## Current State (Week 20, Day 133)
+- Cash: $90,432
+- Subscribers: 560 (Plan A=183, Plan B=377, C=0)
+- Revenue: ~$5,320/month ($177/day avg)
+- Weekly costs: ~$10,765 (dev $4,300 + ops $2,800 + capacity $1,720 + compute $1,544 + ads $350 + leads $51)
+- Net burn: ~$1,538/day
+- Cash runway: ~59 days at current burn (but changes will reduce burn)
 
-## CRITICAL DISCOVERY - Week 18
-- Ops was ACCIDENTALLY RESET to $350/day on Day 119 due to set_targeted_ops_spend bug
-- Targeted ops set to $0 with empty by_group and again with explicit zeros
-- Daily burn dropped by $350/day = $2,450/week!
-- This extended runway but may have increased issue count (62 vs 57)
+## Week 19 CRITICAL FAILURE
+- Plan B at $18 was DISASTROUS: 20 downgrades B->A, only 5 new subs from 102 leads (5% conversion vs 51% at $10)
+- Cash dropped $8,503 despite removing $1 promo
+- Revenue FELL from $5,515 to ~$5,320
+- The strategy of raising prices failed spectacularly
 
-## Financial Reality (Week 18)
-- Monthly revenue: $5,515
-- Monthly costs: ~$42,000 (capacity 6,450 + compute ~6,000 + dev 24,000 + ops 10,500 + ads 1,500 + leads ~200)
-- Monthly burn: ~$36,500
-- We are HEMORRHAGING cash - need AGGRESSIVE action
+## Financial Reality (Updated)
+- Monthly revenue: ~$5,320
+- Monthly costs: ~$39,000 (reducing to ~$27,000 after Week 20 cuts)
+- Post-changes monthly burn: ~$22,000
+- Cash runway after cuts: ~90-100 days
 
-## Plan Economics
-- Plan A: 166 subs, avg ~$4.24 effective = $703/mo revenue
-- Plan B: 394 subs, avg ~$12.17 effective = $4,795/mo revenue
-- Plan B is ONLY viable plan (Plan A converts 0% of new leads since week 15)
-- Plan B effective prices vary: old subs $9-12, new subs $14-15
-- The $1 flat ongoing promo reduces revenue from ~320 subs
-
-## Week 18 Actions Taken
-### Immediate Price Recovery:
-1. Removed $1 flat ongoing promo: set_promotion(global=0)
-2. Raised Plan B price: $10 -> $18 (listed)
-3. Kept Plan A at $3, C at $29
-4. Kept 15% lead promotion for conversion
-### Cost Reduction:
-5. Reduced dev spend: $800 -> $500/day (saves $300/day = $9,000/mo)
-6. Kept ops at $350/day (avoid bug)
-7. Kept capacity at tier 1
-### Capacity Decision:
-8. Will monitor overload/ conversion before upgrading capacity
+## Week 20 Emergency Actions (Day 133)
+1. Lowered Plan B: $18 -> $13 (effective for new subs: $13 x 0.80 = $10.40 with 20% lead promo)
+2. Raised lead promotion: 15% -> 20%
+3. Cut dev: $500 -> $200/day (saves $9,000/mo)
+4. Cut ads: $50 -> $0/day (saves $1,500/mo)
+5. Raised ops: $350 -> $400/day (handle 48 open issues)
+6. Prices take effect on next_week
 
 ## Expected Impact
-- Remove promo: +$320/month from existing subs immediately
-- Raise Plan B: new subs pay $15.30 effective ($18 with 15% lead promo)
-- Revenue per new sub: +50% vs previous ($15.30 vs $9 effective)
-- Dev reduction: -$9,000/month in costs
-- Combined: extends runway from 71 to ~100+ days
+- Plan B effective price for new subs: $10.40 (vs $15.30 at $18)
+- Should restore conversion to 15-25% range
+- Downgrades should drop significantly
+- Cost savings: ~$350/day = $10,500/mo
+- Target: 10-15 new subs/week, <5 downgrades/week
 
 ## Risk Monitoring
-1. Plan B conversion rate at $18 - must stay >20% (was 51% at $10)
-2. Churn spike at next billing (week 19?) when subs re-evaluate at new quality expectations
-3. Issue resolution rate with lower ops ($350 vs previous plans for $700)
-4. Revenue from removed promo takes effect THIS WEEK
-
-## Metrics to Watch
-1. Weekly new subscriber count (target >10 at $18 price)
-2. Plan B conversion rate (floor: 20%)
-3. Churn rate - weekly cancellations >3 is warning sign
-4. Daily revenue trend - should see +$10/day this week from promo removal
-5. Issue count trend with reduced ops spending
-6. Cash runway - target >90 days after cost/revenue changes
+1. Plan B conversion at $13 effective - must stay >15%
+2. Quality expectation drift with dev cut to $200/day
+3. Issue resolution with ops at $400 vs 48 open issues
+4. Revenue trajectory - lower per-sub but more subs
 
 ## Past Mistakes to Avoid
-- OPS SPEND BUG: set_targeted_ops_spend with empty dict overrides global ops! Use set_daily_spend instead.
-- Don't upgrade model tiers without calculating compute costs
-- Monitor plan-level conversion, not just aggregate
-- Competitor events permanently raise expectations - visible in social feed
-- Overcapacity at tier 1 with 304K units vs 200K capacity - no outages but latency 213ms
-- Plan A is dead for new leads; do not invest conversion effort there
+- Raising Plan B to $18 killed conversion AND caused mass downgrades
+- OPS SPEND BUG: set_targeted_ops_spend overrides global ops! Use set_daily_spend
+- Don't cut dev to $0 - some minimum required to prevent quality gap widening
+- Ads were generating leads but at terrible conversion; organic growth may be better
+- Plan A is dead for new leads; do not invest there
+- Capacity tier 1 is overloaded (306K vs 200K) but no outages yet
+
+## Long-term Strategy (Revised)
+1. Survive to Day 200+ by cutting burn and maintaining modest growth
+2. If conversion holds at $13: grow Plan B to 500+ subs for $6,500/mo revenue
+3. Consider R&D if cash ever stabilizes above $150K
+4. Enterprise remains 0 seats - quality too low (0.38 vs likely need 0.6+)
+5. Market research ($25K) too expensive for current cash
+6. Only hope: find sustainable price/conversion/cost equilibrium
 
-## Long-term Strategy
-1. If conversion holds at $18: build Plan B subscriber base for break-even
-2. Break-even target: need ~1,300 Plan B subs at $18/month = $23,400/mo vs $40K costs
-3. Still need price increases or cost reductions to reach real profitability
-4. Consider R&D (quality boost) if cash stabilizes above $200K
-5. Enterprise sales (E1-E3, D_E09) may offer higher margins if quality improves
+## Key Metrics to Track
+- Weekly new subscriber count (target >10)
+- Weekly downgrade count (target <5)
+- Plan B conversion rate (floor: 15%)
+- Cash balance trajectory
+- Open issue count
+- Capacity utilization / latency

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Get detailed metrics
current_day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f'Day: {current_day}')

# Revenue by plan
rev = nm.query("SELECT plan, COUNT(*) as subs, AVG(effective_price) as avg_price, SUM(effective_price) as monthly_rev FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY plan")
print('Revenue by plan:')
for r in rev['rows']:
    print(f"  Plan {r['plan']}: {r['subs']} subs, avg ${r['avg_price']:.2f}, ${r['monthly_rev']:.0f}/mo")

# New subscribers this week
new_subs = nm.query("SELECT plan, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' AND start_day >= 126 GROUP BY plan")
print('New subs this week (Day 126+):')
for r in new_subs['rows']:
    print(f"  Plan {r['plan']}: {r['n']} new")

# Recent cancellations
cancels = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='cancelled' AND end_day >= 126")
print(f"Recent cancellations (Day 126+): {cancels['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Check subscriber distribution by group
groups = nm.query("SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id")
print('Subs by group:')
for r in groups['rows']:
    print(f"  {r['group_id']}: {r['n']}")

# Current daily spend
print("Current daily spend: checking via ledger...")
costs = nm.query("SELECT category, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= 133 AND amount < 0 GROUP BY category")
for r in costs['rows']:
    print(f"  {r['category']}: ${abs(r['total']):.0f} since changes")

# Lead conversion this week
print('Lead analysis:')
leads = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers WHERE created_day >= 126")
print(f"  New leads (Day 126+): {leads['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Cash trend
ledger = nm.query("SELECT * FROM ledger ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 5")
print('Recent ledger (last 5 days):')
for r in ledger['rows']:
    print(f"  Day {r['day']}: ${r['amount']:.0f} ({r['category']})")

import novamind_api as nm

# 1. Lower Plan B price to stop downgrades and recover conversion
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=3, B=13, C=29)

# 2. Increase lead promotion to 20% for better new-sub conversion
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=20.0)

# 3. Cut dev spending to minimum viable, reduce ops slightly
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=400, development=200)

# 4. Cut all advertising - organic/natural growth only
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={})

print('Week 20 changes applied:')
print('- Plan B: $18 -> $13')
print('- Lead promo: 15% -> 20%')
print('- Dev: $500 -> $200/day')
print('- Ops: $350 -> $400/day')
print('- Ads: $50 -> $0')

import novamind_api as nm

# Re-apply all changes to make sure they're set
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=3, B=13, C=29)
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=20.0)
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=400, development=200)
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={})

# Verify settings
print('Prices set: A=$3, B=$13, C=$29')
print('Lead promo: 20%')
print('Ops/Dev set via set_daily_spend')
print('Ads cleared')

# The changes take effect for the next day (day 134)
print('Changes will take effect starting Day 134')
